title: Ensure 'PHP version' is currently supported (if in use)

input_row.title: Ensure 'PHP version' is currently supported (if in use)
input_row.description: Periodically, older versions of PHP may be deprecated and no longer supported. Using a supported version of PHP for App Service apps is recommended to avoid potential unpatched vulnerabilities.
input_row.rationale: Deprecated and unsupported versions of programming and scripting languages can present vulnerabilities which may not be addressed or may not be addressable.
input_row.audit: Take note of the currently supported versions of PHP here: https://www.php.net/supported-versions.php Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, ensure that for a Stack of PHP, the Major version and Minor version reflect a currently supported release. 5. Repeat steps 1-4 for each app. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to get the PHP version: az webapp config show --resource-group --name --query "{LinuxFxVersion:linuxFxVersion,PhpVersion:phpVersion}" If PHP is in use, ensure the version is currently supported. Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list apps: Get-AzWebApp Run the following command to get the app in a resource group with a given name: $app = Get-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName -Name Run the following command to get the PHP version: $app.SiteConfig | select-object LinuxFXVersion, PhpVersion If PHP is in use, ensure the version is currently supported. Repeat for each app. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 7261b898-8a84-4db8-9e04-18527132abb3 - Name: 'App Service apps that use PHP should use a specified 'PHP version''
input_row.remediation: Note: No action is required if PHP is not in use. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, for a Stack of PHP, set the Major version and Minor version to a currently supported release. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to list supported runtimes: az webapp list-runtimes For each app requiring remediation, run the following command with the appropriate parameters to update the PHP version: az webapp config set --resource-group --name [--linux-fx-version ][--php-version ] Remediate from PowerShell For each app requiring remediation, run the following command to update the PHP version: Set-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName -Name - phpVersion Note: Currently, there is no way to update an app's Linux FX Version setting using PowerShell.
input_row.impact: If your app is written using version-dependent features or libraries, they may not be available on more recent versions. If you wish to update, research the impact thoroughly.
input_row.default_value: The version is selected during creation.

step1_initial_assessment:
### AUDIT APPROACH: Manual

### MANUAL STEPS:
1. **Access the AWS Management Console**: 
   - Navigate to the AWS Management Console -> Open the Amazon Elastic Beanstalk service (or AWS Lambda service depending on your application stack).
   
2. **Select the Application**: 
   - In Elastic Beanstalk, choose the relevant application and then select the environment you want to check. In the case of Lambda functions, locate the specific function that is utilizing PHP.

3. **Check the PHP Version**: 
   - For Elastic Beanstalk: Go to the Configuration page of the environment. 
   - For Lambda Functions: You can view the configuration by clicking on the function name in the console.
   
4. **Validation**: 
   - Check the runtime that is configured. Ensure that the PHP version aligns with the currently supported versions found on the PHP's official supported versions page: [https://www.php.net/supported-versions.php](https://www.php.net/supported-versions.php). 

5. **Expected Secure Configuration**: 
   - The configured PHP version in your application should match or be greater than the minimum version listed as supported on the PHP website.

6. **Confirm Compliance**: 
   - Document the PHP versions in use for each application. If any application is using a deprecated or unsupported version, note it down for remediation. Repeat the steps for each application in your environment where PHP may be utilized.

### ADDITIONAL INFORMAION:
This process must be repeated periodically to ensure compliance with updates in PHP versions and to maintain a secure application environment. 

### IMPACT:
Using an unsupported version of PHP can lead to vulnerabilities not being addressed, which can increase security risks for applications running on that version. Always ensure your applications are running on supported software versions.
